text: Closures in JavaScript work because functions remember the scope in which they were created. Think of it as a backpack the function carries with its variables. 1. Define a function inside another. 2. Return the inner function. See MDN docs for more.

text: Promises represent a value that may be available now, in the future, or never. Because they model async operations, you can chain them with .then(). Similar to a ticket at a deli — you get a number and come back when your order is ready. Check the spec for full semantics.
